title: Review-reports directory creation timing unclear in RULES.md Step 3b
description: RULES.md Step 3b doesn't specify when to create .beads/agent-summaries/{epic-id}/review-reports/ relative to spawning Pantry (review mode). reviews.md says 'before composing review prompts' but RULES.md doesn't mention it. Add explicit directory creation to RULES.md Step 3b.

title: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md bd close ordering creates failure window before summary doc
description:
Root cause: The 'bd close {TASK_ID}' instruction in dirt-pusher-skeleton.md appears as a standalone line after the 6-step numbered list (line 42), structurally disconnected from the step sequence. The instruction is ambiguous about when it executes relative to Step 6 (write summary doc). If an agent closes the bead before writing the summary doc and then crashes, the task is marked closed with no summary artifact. Checkpoint B (checkpoints.md:246) requires reading the summary doc and would find nothing, but the task is already closed.

Affected surfaces:
- dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:42 -- 'After committing: bd close {TASK_ID}' appears orphaned after step list

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 10, P3), Edge Cases (Finding 6, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer rated P2 due to the failure window between bead closure and summary doc creation. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Move 'bd close {TASK_ID}' to an explicit position after Step 6 with a note: 'Close the bead ONLY after the summary doc is successfully written.' Consider making it Step 7.

Acceptance criteria:
- bd close instruction is explicitly sequenced after summary doc write (Step 6)
- Either incorporated into a numbered step or clearly labeled as post-Step-6
- No ambiguity about when bead closure occurs relative to artifact creation

title: Checkpoints epic ID resolution table is overspecified and ambiguous
description: checkpoints.md provides an epic ID resolution table but mixes epic ID format (3-char) with task ID patterns (full format). Does not clearly handle standalone tasks (e.g. hs_website-596y with no .N suffix). Simplify to plain English with annotated examples.

title: checkpoints.md CCB lists report paths twice (duplication risk)
description:
Root cause: The CCB (Checkpoint C) template in checkpoints.md lists the 4 individual report paths in both the 'Individual reports' section (lines 383-387) and the 'Check 0: Report Existence Verification' section (lines 392-396). The duplication means path format changes must be updated in two places within the same template.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:383-387 -- Individual reports section
- checkpoints.md:392-396 -- Check 0 section (duplicate listing)

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 8, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 maintainability polish.

Fix: In Check 0, reference 'the 4 individual report paths listed above' instead of re-listing them.

Acceptance criteria:
- Report paths appear only once in the CCB template, with Check 0 referencing the earlier listing

title: Scout doesn't explain _standalone epic lifecycle
description: RULES.md says _standalone persists across sessions (not cleaned up with _session-* artifacts) but Scout template has no mention of this. A fresh Scout might group _standalone tasks from different sessions incorrectly. Add clarification to scout.md about _standalone being persistent.

title: pantry.md Review Mode does not generate Big Head preview file for CCO audit
description:
Root cause: pantry.md Review Mode Step 5 creates preview files for the 4 Nitpicker review types by combining nitpicker-skeleton.md with each data file. However, no preview is generated for Big Head's consolidation prompt. If CCO is meant to audit all prompts before team creation, the Big Head prompt is excluded from preview-based auditing.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:137-146 -- Step 5 (Write Combined Review Previews) omits Big Head

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 9, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 completeness item.

Fix: Either (a) add a Step 5b to create a Big Head preview, or (b) explicitly document that Big Head's prompt is excluded from CCO preview audit and explain why.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either a Big Head preview is generated, or the exclusion is explicitly documented with rationale

title: SESSION_DIR variable not quoted in shell mkdir command in RULES.md
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 115 uses mkdir -p ${SESSION_DIR}/{task-metadata,previews,prompts} without quoting ${SESSION_DIR}. While the value is a hash (no spaces), shell best practice is to quote variable expansions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- unquoted ${SESSION_DIR} in mkdir command

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 best-practice polish item.

Fix: Change to mkdir -p "${SESSION_DIR}"/{task-metadata,previews,prompts}

Acceptance criteria:
- SESSION_DIR is quoted in the mkdir command

title: Queen information diet wording ambiguous about agent data files
description: RULES.md Information Diet says 'DO NOT READ: source code, tests, data files, configs...' but dirt-pusher data files are also 'data files'. A fresh Queen might think she cannot read Pantry verdict tables or preview paths. Clarify: the prohibition is on project data files, not orchestration artifacts like data files and verdict tables.

title: RULES.md pantry.md section references not explicit
description: pantry-impl.md references 'pantry.md, Section 1' and pantry-review.md references 'pantry.md, Section 2', but RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b just say '→ templates/pantry.md' without section numbers. Add section references to RULES.md for traceability.

title: RULES.md retry counter interaction between per-checkpoint and session-total limits is unspecified
description:
Root cause: The retry limits table in RULES.md (lines 150-151) says Checkpoint C failures allow 1 retry, and a session total of 5 retries. But it does not specify whether a Checkpoint C re-run (which may spawn a new Big Head instance) counts toward the session total. The interaction between per-checkpoint limits and the global session cap is ambiguous.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:150-151 -- Retry limits table, Checkpoint C row

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 5, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is an edge case in retry accounting, not a functional failure.

Fix: Clarify in RULES.md retry table: 'Each Checkpoint C re-run counts as 1 toward both the per-checkpoint limit (1) and the session total (5).'

Acceptance criteria:
- Retry table explicitly states how per-checkpoint retries interact with the session total

title: Pantry told to read implementation.md but no explanation of what to extract
description: pantry.md Step 1 says 'Read this file: implementation.md' but gives no guidance on what data to extract or how it shapes the Pantry's output. A fresh Pantry agent would read it and not know what to do with it. Clarify: is it informational (absorb the policy) or extractive (pull specific data)?

title: Multi-epic checkpoint artifact naming undefined
description: checkpoints.md says 'Use multi for consolidations spanning multiple epics' but RULES.md doesn't mention multi-epic scenarios. A fresh Pest Control wouldn't know when to use 'multi' vs individual epic IDs. Clarify whether multi-epic reviews are supported and document the naming convention.

title: Dirt-pusher-skeleton uses {TASK_ID} placeholder but doesn't define it
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md defines {TASK_TYPE}, {AGENT_TYPE}, {DATA_FILE_PATH} in its placeholder list but uses {TASK_ID} in the commit message template without defining it. A fresh Queen filling in the skeleton would not know what value to substitute. Add {TASK_ID} to the placeholder definitions.

title: Explore tmux-based agent spawning for real-time observability
description: Explore running orchestrated subagents in tmux windows instead of background Task agents, so you can observe agent work in real-time. Key research questions: (1) Can the boss-bot launch claude CLI sessions in tmux windows via Bash? (2) How to pipe prompts into those sessions? (3) How to collect results back? (4) Hook-based automation options? Goal: Each spawned agent gets its own tmux window you can switch to with ctrl+b n/p. May influence orchestration templates if feasible.

title: Review timestamp ownership contradicts between pantry.md and checkpoints.md
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 2 says the Pantry generates the review timestamp. checkpoints.md says the Queen generates it. A fresh Pantry and a fresh Pest Control would disagree on who owns this value. Pick one owner, document it, and update both files.

title: Review type names inconsistent between reviews.md and file naming
description: reviews.md section titles use 'Correctness Redux', 'Edge Cases (P2)' etc. but file names and checkpoints use 'correctness', 'edge-cases'. Drop 'Redux' and priority annotations from section titles to match the canonical short names used in file naming.

title: Checkpoint A.5 relies on Queen-provided file list with no independent scope validation
description:
Root cause: Checkpoint A.5 (checkpoints.md:189) uses {list files from task description} as the expected file list, provided by the Queen. If the Queen passes an incomplete or incorrect list, A.5 will produce false positives or false negatives for scope violations. The checkpoint has no independent way to derive expected scope.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:189 -- Checkpoint A.5 expected file list placeholder

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 4, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because this is a known limitation of the input-passing design, not a bug.

Fix: Either have Pest Control cross-reference against the original data file at a known path, or document this as a known limitation: 'A.5 accuracy depends on the Queen file list -- review WARN verdicts carefully.'

Acceptance criteria:
- Either A.5 has an independent scope reference, or the limitation is explicitly documented

title: RULES.md Queen Prohibitions still says 'data files' instead of 'project data files' (ant-farm-6jv gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit c3771df (ant-farm-6jv) disambiguated 'data files' in the Information Diet section (line 80) to 'project data files' with an explanatory note. However, the Queen Prohibitions section at RULES.md line 6 still reads 'data files' without the 'project' qualifier. A Queen reading the Prohibitions section first (as instructed) encounters the ambiguity that ant-farm-6jv was meant to fix, before reaching the clarification 74 lines later.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:6 -- 'NEVER read source code, tests, data files, or config files' should say 'project data files'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 3, P2), Excellence (Finding 4, P3)
Taking highest severity: P2. Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates ant-farm-6jv acceptance criteria.

Fix: Change 'data files' to 'project data files' on line 6 of RULES.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- RULES.md line 6 reads 'project data files' instead of 'data files'
- Consistent with the Information Diet section disambiguation

title: big-head-skeleton.md wiring Step 3 assumes Queen can SendMessage to team members
description:
Root cause: The wiring instructions in big-head-skeleton.md Step 3 (lines 32-43) tell the Queen to use SendMessage(to='big-head', message=...) after the 4 Nitpickers finish. However, in Claude Code's team model, the Queen (team lead/parent) may not be able to send messages to team members via SendMessage. The fallback on lines 42-43 -- 'Big Head can also discover them from the brief if SendMessage is delayed' -- partially mitigates this, but the primary mechanism documented in Step 3 may not function as described.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:32-43 -- wiring Step 3 SendMessage instruction

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but architectural in nature. P2 because if the primary communication mechanism does not work, Big Head relies entirely on the fallback path.

Fix: Clarify the actual communication mechanism. Options: (a) make the data file the primary mechanism and remove or demote the SendMessage step, (b) have a Nitpicker reviewer forward paths to Big Head after completion, (c) document that the brief file IS the primary mechanism and SendMessage is supplementary. Whichever approach is chosen, document it as canonical.

Acceptance criteria:
- The primary mechanism for Big Head to receive report paths is clearly documented
- No reliance on a communication pattern that may not be supported by Claude Code's team model
- The fallback path (brief-based discovery) is either promoted to primary or explicitly documented as the backup

title: Timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS repeated 5+ times across files
description: The timestamp format is redefined in checkpoints.md at lines 31, 97, 152, 277, 443 and in pantry.md line 101. If the format ever changes, all 5+ locations must be updated. Define once at the top of checkpoints.md and reference it elsewhere.

title: Add glossary to RULES.md defining bead, epic, wave, dirt pusher
description: Terms like 'bead', 'epic', 'wave', 'dirt pusher', 'nitpicker' are used throughout orchestration files but never defined. A fresh agent must infer meaning from context. Add a Terminology section to RULES.md with concise definitions.

title: Epic artifact directory creation not enforced in RULES.md
description: RULES.md says the Queen creates epic directories at Step 2 but provides no explicit timing relative to Pantry spawn. reviews.md separately says to create review-reports/ before composing review prompts. A fresh Queen must manually create directories at different times with no consolidated checklist. Add a pre-spawn directory setup block to RULES.md Steps 2 and 3b.

title: Session directory passing mechanism not shown in RULES.md
description: RULES.md Step 0 generates SESSION_ID and creates the session dir. Scout.md expects {SESSION_DIR} as input. But RULES.md doesn't show HOW the Queen passes the session dir path to the Scout or any other agent. Add explicit instruction: pass session dir as a parameter in the spawn prompt.

title: big-head-skeleton.md: CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH missing leading dot in .beads/ path
description:
Root cause: Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md defines {CONSOLIDATED_OUTPUT_PATH} as 'beads/agent-summaries/...' instead of '.beads/agent-summaries/...'. The missing leading dot means the Queen would substitute a path that writes the consolidated report to a non-existent 'beads/' directory at the repo root instead of the canonical '.beads/' hidden directory.

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- placeholder definition uses 'beads/' instead of '.beads/'

Contributing reviews: Correctness (Finding 1, P2), Excellence (Finding 1, P2)
Both reviewers independently identified this and cross-referenced each other. Confirmed as a genuine path error that would cause wrong-directory writes at fill-time.

Fix: Change 'beads/agent-summaries/' to '.beads/agent-summaries/' on line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Line 14 of big-head-skeleton.md starts with '.beads/agent-summaries/'
- The path matches the convention used in all other orchestration files

title: Placeholder syntax inconsistent across orchestration files
description: Orchestration files mix three placeholder syntaxes: {curly-lowercase} for substitution, {UPPERCASE} for substitution, and <angle-brackets> for substitution. A fresh agent cannot tell which are literal vs to-be-substituted. Standardize: {UPPERCASE} = substitute, document convention at top of each file that uses placeholders.

title: Big Head data file structure not documented in pantry.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain but doesn't show the exact markdown format. The structure lives in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol. A fresh Pantry might produce the wrong format. Either inline the format or add an explicit cross-reference.

title: Pest Control CCO review section doesn't specify where commit range comes from
description: checkpoints.md CCO section for review prompts says 'Run git diff --name-only <first-commit>..<last-commit>' but doesn't explain how Pest Control receives these values. A fresh Pest Control agent would waste time looking for the source. Add: 'The Queen provides the commit range in the spawn prompt.'

title: Task ID variable naming inconsistent across templates
description: Some files use {task-id-suffix}, some use {task-id}, some use {TASK_ID}. These may refer to the same value or different values. A fresh agent cannot tell. Standardize variable names: pick one convention and find-replace across all templates.

title: checkpoints.md dangling cross-reference to non-existent 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' section
description:
Root cause: Three locations in checkpoints.md reference a section titled 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' that does not exist in the file. The closest match is 'Pest Control Overview' (line 9). The dangling cross-references appear at lines 67, 243, and 300.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:67 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint A prompt
- checkpoints.md:243 -- Dirt Pusher Checkpoint B prompt
- checkpoints.md:300 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint B prompt

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 7, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 as this is a documentation inconsistency that could confuse Pest Control but does not break functionality.

Fix: Either (a) rename 'Pest Control Overview' section to 'Pest Control: The Verification Subagent' to match the references, or (b) update the three references to say 'Pest Control Overview'.

Acceptance criteria:
- The section name referenced at lines 67, 243, and 300 matches an actual section heading in checkpoints.md

title: big-head-skeleton.md not updated during terminology standardization (ant-farm-ss6 gap)
description:
Root cause: Commit 03f6299 (ant-farm-ss6) standardized task ID terminology across orchestration templates, adding canonical term definitions blocks and normalizing placeholder naming. big-head-skeleton.md was missed. Three separate issues stem from this single omission:

Affected surfaces:
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses {epic-id} (lowercase/hyphenated) instead of {EPIC_ID} (uppercase/underscored)
- big-head-skeleton.md:14 -- uses <timestamp> (angle brackets) instead of {timestamp} (curly braces)
- big-head-skeleton.md:1-47 -- missing the canonical term definitions block present in pantry.md:5-8, checkpoints.md:4-7, dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:8-11

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Findings 1,2,3 -- P3), Correctness (Finding 2 -- P2), Excellence (Findings 2,3 -- P3)
Correctness reviewer rated P2 because it directly violates the acceptance criteria of ant-farm-ss6. Taking the highest severity: P2.

Fix: (1) Add canonical term definitions block after line 6 of big-head-skeleton.md. (2) Change {epic-id} to {EPIC_ID} on line 14. (3) Change <timestamp> to {timestamp} on line 14.

Acceptance criteria:
- big-head-skeleton.md contains the same term definitions block as pantry.md, checkpoints.md, and dirt-pusher-skeleton.md
- All placeholders use {UPPERCASE} naming convention
- All timestamp references use {timestamp} (curly braces)

title: MANDATORY keyword formatting inconsistent across templates
description: implementation.md uses plain 'MANDATORY', dirt-pusher-skeleton uses 'MANDATORY keyword present', checkpoints uses '(MANDATORY GATE)'. No consistent formatting. Pick one style (e.g. **MANDATORY**) and standardize.

title: Epic ID vs epic suffix terminology mixed across files
description: Some places say 'epic ID' (e.g. RULES.md), some say 'epic suffix' (e.g. checkpoints.md line 29: 'Use 3-char epic suffix'). These refer to the same value (e.g. 74g). Pick one term and standardize.

title: Find a better name for Pantry 'data files'
description: The Pantry template references 'data files' — find a more descriptive/thematic name that fits the ant-farm metaphor.

title: prompts/ directory creation is redundant between RULES.md Step 0 and pantry.md Review Mode
description:
Root cause: RULES.md Step 0 (line 86/115) creates the prompts/ directory via brace expansion in mkdir. pantry.md Review Mode Step 3 (line 107/110) also says 'Create the prompts directory if needed.' The redundancy is harmless (mkdir -p is idempotent) but creates confusion about who owns directory creation.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:115 -- mkdir -p includes prompts/ via brace expansion
- pantry.md:107-110 -- 'Create the prompts directory if needed'

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 11, P3), Correctness (Finding 7, P3)
Both reviewers independently identified the same redundancy. P3 because the behavior is correct either way.

Fix: Keep both (belt-and-suspenders) but add a comment in pantry.md: 'The Queen pre-creates this directory at Step 0, but create if needed as a safety net.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The intentional redundancy is documented with a comment in pantry.md

title: Pantry review return format doesn't show Big Head consolidation file
description: pantry.md Section 2 Step 6 return table shows 4 review types but the Big Head consolidation data file path is listed separately below the table. Big-head-skeleton.md Step 0 says 'Read your consolidation brief from {DATA_FILE_PATH}'. A fresh Queen might miss the consolidation file path when parsing Pantry output. Make it prominent.

title: Big Head spawning mechanics unclear for fresh Queen
description: big-head-skeleton.md says Big Head is a team member (NOT a separate Task agent) but uses {PLACEHOLDER} syntax from Task tool templates. A fresh Queen would not know how to wire this through TeamCreate + SendMessage. Add explicit Queen-facing instructions showing the TeamCreate → SendMessage workflow with an example.

title: checkpoints.md {checkpoint} placeholder not defined in term definitions block
description:
Root cause: The filename pattern on line 20 of checkpoints.md uses {checkpoint} as a placeholder, but this is not defined in the term definitions block (lines 4-7). While its meaning is inferable from context, it breaks the convention of all placeholders being explicitly defined.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:20 -- {checkpoint} used in filename pattern without definition

Contributing reviews: Excellence (Finding 7, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 consistency polish.

Fix: Add a brief definition: '{checkpoint} is the checkpoint abbreviation: cco, wwd, dmvdc, or ccb'

Acceptance criteria:
- {checkpoint} is defined in the term definitions block or has an explanatory note

title: Task ID suffix terminology inconsistent across templates
description: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md uses <task-id>, pantry.md uses {task-id-suffix}, checkpoints.md uses both interchangeably. A fresh agent cannot tell if it should use the full ID (hs_website-74g.1) or just the suffix (74g.1). Standardize to one term across all orchestration files.

title: checkpoints.md Checkpoint A Nitpickers uses 0-based numbering (0-6) while Dirt Pushers uses 1-based (1-7)
description:
Root cause: Within checkpoints.md, the Checkpoint A section for Nitpickers numbers its 7 checks as 0-6, while the Checkpoint A section for Dirt Pushers numbers its 7 checks as 1-7. Both claim '7 checks' (correct count), but the inconsistent indexing style within the same file and same checkpoint type creates confusion and could cause off-by-one reporting errors if Pest Control references checks by number.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:154 -- Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist uses 0-based indexing (0-6)
- (Contrast: Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A at line 96 uses 1-based indexing 1-7)

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 6, P2)
Single reviewer finding but rated P2 due to the potential for off-by-one errors in verification reporting.

Fix: Renumber the Nitpickers Checkpoint A checklist from 1-7 to match the Dirt Pushers Checkpoint A convention.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both Checkpoint A sections (Dirt Pushers and Nitpickers) use 1-based numbering
- Check count text ('7 checks') matches the actual numbered items

title: Session ID collision risk from truncated shasum with epoch-second input
description:
Root cause: RULES.md line 85 generates session IDs via 'date +%s | shasum | head -c 6' which produces a 6-char hex string. The input is epoch seconds, so two Queens started within the same second would produce identical session IDs, causing artifact directory collisions.

Affected surfaces:
- RULES.md:85 -- Session ID generation command

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Finding 1, P3)
Single reviewer finding. P3 because concurrent Queens in the same repo are already implied to be unsupported by concurrency rules.

Fix: Either add per-invocation randomness (e.g., '(date +%s%N; echo $$) | shasum | head -c 6') or document that concurrent Queens are unsupported.

Acceptance criteria:
- Either the session ID has sufficient entropy to avoid same-second collisions, or the single-Queen constraint is explicitly documented

title: Missing prompts/ directory creation before Pantry writes
description: Pantry writes data files to {session-dir}/prompts/ but nobody creates that directory. RULES.md Step 0 creates {session-dir}/{task-metadata,previews} but prompts/ is not included. A fresh Pantry agent would fail on its first write attempt. Fix: add prompts/ to the mkdir -p command in RULES.md Step 0, or have Pantry create it.

title: Checkpoints CCO FAIL verdict format has no example
description: checkpoints.md shows PASS verdict format but no example of a FAIL verdict with specific check failures listed. A fresh Pest Control agent might format FAIL output incorrectly. Add a FAIL example showing check number, name, and evidence.

title: checkpoints.md sampling formula contradicts 'minimum 3' prose text
description:
Root cause: checkpoints.md line 311 specifies the formula min(5, ceil(N/3)) for selecting findings to spot-check, but the same line states 'minimum 3, or all findings if fewer than 3'. The formula can produce values below 3 -- e.g., for N=6, ceil(6/3)=2, then min(5,2)=2. The English text says minimum 3 but the formula does not enforce this floor. Pest Control could either under-sample (following formula) or ignore the formula (following prose), creating verification ambiguity.

Affected surfaces:
- checkpoints.md:311 -- formula/prose contradiction in Checkpoint B Nitpickers sampling rule

Contributing reviews: Clarity (Finding 8, P3 with cross-ref to edge-cases), Edge Cases (Finding 10, P2)
Edge-cases reviewer confirmed this as a genuine behavioral risk at P2. Taking highest: P2.

Fix: Replace min(5, ceil(N/3)) with max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) to enforce the stated minimum of 3. Rewrite the prose in plain English: 'Pick at least 3 findings (or all findings if fewer than 3 exist), up to a maximum of 5.'

Acceptance criteria:
- The formula on line 311 enforces a floor of 3
- The English description matches the formula exactly
- No contradiction between formula and prose

title: Wave concept used in RULES.md but never defined
description: RULES.md Hard Gates table references 'Next agent in wave' and checkpoints.md says 'BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave'. The concept of a wave (batch of parallel agents) is never defined. Add to glossary: 'Wave: a batch of agents spawned in parallel. Wave N completes before Wave N+1 begins.'

title: Big Head data file format split between pantry.md and reviews.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain (4 report paths, dedup protocol, bead filing instructions, consolidated output path) but the actual format/structure is in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. A fresh Pantry would need to cross-reference but there's no explicit pointer. Add cross-reference from pantry.md Step 4 to reviews.md.

title: Templates lack explicit guards for missing or empty input artifacts
description:
Root cause: Multiple templates assume their input artifacts exist and are well-formed without specifying explicit error behavior when they are missing or empty. This is a systematic gap across the template suite -- each template handles the happy path but does not specify what to write or return when a required input is absent.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:26 -- No validation that task-metadata files exist before reading (Step 2)
- pantry.md:26-32 -- No handling for empty or malformed metadata fields (e.g., feature tasks with no 'Root cause')
- pantry.md:94 -- Review mode has no handling for empty changed-file list
- big-head-skeleton.md:23 -- Step 1 says 'FAIL immediately if any missing' but does not specify where to write a failure artifact
- checkpoints.md:259 -- No specification for handling bd show failures in Checkpoint B

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Findings 2, 3, 7, 8, 9 -- all P3)
All five findings share the same pattern: expected input artifact may be missing/empty, and the template does not specify explicit error behavior. P3 because each individual case is unlikely but the pattern is systemic.

Fix: Establish a convention across all templates: 'If an expected input artifact is missing or empty, write a brief failure artifact to the expected output path explaining the issue, then return FAIL with error details.' Apply this pattern to Pantry (missing/malformed metadata, empty file list), Big Head (missing reports), and Checkpoints (bd show failures).

Acceptance criteria:
- Each template has explicit instructions for handling missing/empty inputs
- Failure artifacts are written to expected output paths so downstream consumers are not left guessing
- Infrastructure failures (tool unavailability) are distinguished from substance failures (agent quality)
